name: Lexington Law
description_short: Attorney-led credit repair firm with legal expertise in consumer credit law. One of the most recognized names in credit repair, now operating under a restructured model.
description_long:
Lexington Law is one of the most widely recognized names in the credit repair industry, known for its attorney-led approach to disputing negative items on credit reports. Originally founded as a law firm specializing in consumer credit issues, Lexington Law leverages legal expertise that most credit repair companies simply do not have. Their team includes paralegals and attorneys who understand the nuances of the Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA), the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA), and other consumer protection statutes that govern how creditors and bureaus must handle disputes.

The company was restructured in 2004 and has undergone several operational changes over the years, including a notable FTC action in 2023 that resulted in significant business changes. Despite these challenges, Lexington Law continues to operate and serve clients across all 50 states. Their current service model offers two primary tiers — Concord Standard and Concord Premier — with the Premier plan adding features like FICO score tracking, real-time alerts, and creditor interventions that go beyond basic bureau disputes.

Where Lexington Law genuinely excels is in handling complex credit situations that involve legal gray areas — disputes where a creditor is technically in violation of reporting requirements, cases involving mixed credit files, or situations where identity theft has led to fraudulent accounts. Having attorneys review these cases can make a meaningful difference in outcomes compared to template-based dispute services. The company also offers an online portal where clients can review their case status, view documents, and communicate with their legal team.

However, there are legitimate concerns that prospective clients should weigh. Lexington Law's BBB profile is currently Not Rated and under review by the BBB as of early 2026, reflecting unresolved complaints and the fallout from regulatory actions. They do not offer a money-back guarantee, which means you are paying monthly regardless of results. Their pricing has also increased in recent years, with the addition of a setup fee that was previously waived. For consumers with straightforward credit issues — late payments, collections, or incorrect balances — a less expensive service may deliver comparable results without the premium price tag associated with legal representation.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee. Services are billed monthly with no refund policy for completed work.

tiers.name: Concord Standard
features: Bureau disputes with all three bureaus

diagnosis: Lexington Law is best suited for consumers dealing with complex credit issues that may involve legal violations — mixed credit files, identity theft accounts, or creditors reporting inaccurately under the FCRA. The attorney-led approach genuinely adds value for these situations compared to template-based dispute services. However, the company carries significant baggage: a BBB profile currently under review, a 2023 FTC regulatory action, no money-back guarantee, and higher pricing ($99.95-$129.95/mo plus $89.95 setup) than many competitors offering comparable basic dispute services. For consumers with straightforward credit issues like late payments or simple collections, a less expensive service will likely deliver similar results without the premium price tag.
services: Cease and desist letters to collectors
Legal analysis of credit report violations
typical_results_timeline: Initial dispute responses typically arrive within 30-45 days. Most clients see notable improvements within 4-6 months, though complex legal cases may take longer as attorney-led strategies are more thorough.
